# PROPOSAL VAULT
# File: upwork-auto-apply/assets/PROPOSAL_VAULT.md
# Version: 1.0.3
#
# This file stores proven proposal hooks and structures.
# ProposalAI reads it at the start of every session.
# New entries are added automatically by the Memory Promotion Protocol
# when you report an interview or hire.
#
# Do not edit the promoted entries manually — they are added by ProposalAI.
# You may edit the seed entries below to match your own voice.

---

## HOW TO READ THIS FILE

Each entry has:
- HOOK: The opening lines that proved effective
- STRUCTURE: The framework used in the full proposal
- CONTEXT: What type of job this worked for
- PERFORMANCE: Interview or Hired
- PROMOTED ON: Date added to vault
- NOTES: Why it worked (extracted by ProposalAI)

---

## CATEGORY: WEB DEVELOPMENT

---

HOOK ID: WD-001
Performance:   Hired
Budget range:  $3,000 to $8,000
Promoted on:   [initial seed]
Source job ID: SEED

HOOK TEXT:
You need this live before [deadline], and you have probably already spoken to
developers who said two months minimum. Here is how I would get it done in three weeks.

STRUCTURE:
Hook:        Address their timeline pain directly before anything else
Credibility: "I shipped [similar project] for [client type] in [X days]"
Preview:     Name three specific technical decisions that compress the timeline
CTA:         "If that sounds reasonable, I have 20 minutes this week to map it out with you."

NOTES:
Works because it names the competitor problem (slow developers) before naming the solution.
The credibility line must include a specific delivery time, not just the word "fast."
Best for: MVP builds, product launches, deadline-critical projects.

---

HOOK ID: WD-002
Performance:   Interview
Budget range:  $500 to $2,000
Promoted on:   [initial seed]
Source job ID: SEED

HOOK TEXT:
Three things in your brief immediately told me this is not a standard build:
[observation 1], [observation 2], and [observation 3].
That combination requires an approach most developers miss on the first pass.

STRUCTURE:
Hook:        Prove deep reading by naming three specific details from the brief
Credibility: "I have built this exact architecture for [client context]"
Preview:     Name the specific technical issue and how you would solve it
CTA:         "Worth a quick call to confirm we are thinking about this the same way?"

NOTES:
Very effective for technical clients who are suspicious of generic proposals.
The three observations MUST come from the actual job description — never fabricate them.
Fill in the [observation] placeholders before sending.

---

## CATEGORY: AUTOMATION AND SCRIPTING

---

HOOK ID: AU-001
Performance:   Hired
Budget range:  $500 to $3,000
Promoted on:   [initial seed]
Source job ID: SEED

HOOK TEXT:
The task you are describing takes your team [estimated time] every week.
After automation, it should take zero.
I have done exactly this for [N] clients — here is the architecture I would use for yours.

STRUCTURE:
Hook:        Quantify the time waste they are currently experiencing
Credibility: "Automated [similar workflow] for [client type], saving [X hours per week]"
Preview:     Name the specific tool or stack and a two-step approach
CTA:         "I can have a working prototype in front of you by [Day plus 3]. Interested?"

NOTES:
The time quantification in the hook does the heavy lifting.
If the job does not mention time wasted, estimate conservatively based on the task.
Best for: data pipelines, report generation, scraping, CRM automation.

---

HOOK ID: AU-002
Performance:   Interview
Budget range:  $200 to $1,000
Promoted on:   [initial seed]
Source job ID: SEED

HOOK TEXT:
Quick question before I write the proposal: are you open to a solution that is
40 percent cheaper to maintain long-term, or does the stack need to stay within
what you have listed? I ask because I have two approaches — one conventional, one better.

STRUCTURE:
Hook:        Open with a strategic question that signals deep expertise
Credibility: Brief note on why you are asking (you have seen this decision matter before)
Preview:     Offer both approaches in three bullet points each
CTA:         "Happy to walk through the tradeoffs in 15 minutes."

NOTES:
Counter-intuitive: starting with a question creates immediate dialogue.
Use only when the job reveals a technical choice the client may not have considered.
Risk: some clients want answers, not questions — use for sophisticated clients only.

---

## CATEGORY: API INTEGRATION

---

HOOK ID: API-001
Performance:   Hired
Budget range:  $1,000 to $5,000
Promoted on:   [initial seed]
Source job ID: SEED

HOOK TEXT:
I have integrated [relevant API] before.
The part that surprises most developers is [specific technical gotcha].
Here is how I handle it to make sure your integration does not break at scale.

STRUCTURE:
Hook:        Name a specific technical gotcha that proves deep API knowledge
Credibility: "Built [X] integrations with [similar system] for [client type]"
Preview:     Outline error handling, rate limiting, and edge case strategy in three points
CTA:         "I can start today — want to see a quick proof of concept first?"

NOTES:
The gotcha must be real and specific. Research the API for 10 minutes before sending.
If you do not know the API, use WD-002 instead.

---

## CATEGORY: DATA AND ANALYTICS

---

HOOK ID: DA-001
Performance:   Interview
Budget range:  $500 to $4,000
Promoted on:   [initial seed]
Source job ID: SEED

HOOK TEXT:
Most dashboards show you what happened.
What you are describing sounds like you need to know what is about to happen.
That is a different build — let me show you what it looks like.

STRUCTURE:
Hook:        Reframe their problem at a higher level of sophistication
Credibility: "Built predictive dashboards for [N] companies in [industry]"
Preview:     Name two metrics you would prioritize and why, based on the job description
CTA:         "If you want to see what the MVP looks like, I can mock it up in 24 hours."

NOTES:
Works when the job focuses on reporting but the real need is decision-making.
Elevates the conversation from commodity (build me a chart) to strategic (what should I measure).

---

## PROMOTED ENTRIES

New entries are added here automatically when you run the Memory Promotion Protocol.
Format: same as seed entries above.

[ProposalAI appends promoted hooks below this line]

---

## ANTI-PATTERNS — NEVER USE THESE OPENINGS

These have been tested and consistently fail. ProposalAI will never use them.

- "Hi, I am [Name] and I am very interested in your project..."
- "I have X years of experience in..."
- "I would love to work with you on this exciting opportunity..."
- "I am a fast learner who pays attention to detail..."
- "Please review my portfolio at..." (put this at the end, never the start)
- "I can do this for cheaper than others..."
- "I have read your job description carefully..." (show it, do not say it)
